[825]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 龍護高家堰 - Literal phrase meaning 'the dragon protected the Gaojiayan embankment.' Likely refers to a supernatural event where a dragon is said to have safeguarded the embankment.
  • 學使李公因培 - Refers to 'Mr. Li Yinpei, the provincial examiner,' indicating his official title and name.
  • 科考淮安 - Means 'administered the provincial examination in Huai'an,' referring to his role in conducting the exam.
  • 清晨，風雨怒號 - Vivid description of 'early morning, with fierce winds and rain howling,' evoking a dramatic atmosphere.
  • 生徒驚顧 - Means 'the students were startled and looked around,' describing their reaction to the storm.
  • 不能唱名 - Means 'unable to call out names,' indicating the disruption caused by the storm.
  • 地大震 - Refers to 'a massive earthquake,' describing the natural disaster.
  • 旗竿，被龍攫入雲中 - Means 'the flagpole was grabbed by a dragon and pulled into the clouds,' a fantastical description of the event.
  • 河水暴涨 - Means 'the river water surged,' indicating the rise in water levels.
  • 與高家堰相齊 - Means 'reached the level of the Gaojiayan embankment,' describing the height of the flood.
  • 河督高公及各廳官 - Refers to 'Governor Gao and the officials of various bureaus,' indicating the high-ranking officials present.
  • 面如土色 - Means 'their faces turned ashen,' describing their fear.
  • 皆云西風一大，則淮揚休矣 - Means 'all said that if the west wind intensified, Huaiyang would be doomed,' indicating the dire predictions.
  • 忽轉東風 - Means 'suddenly the wind turned east,' describing a change in weather.
  • 天低若蓋 - Means 'the sky seemed to lower like a lid,' evoking a sense of impending doom.
  • 將壓人頭 - Means 'about to press down on people's heads,' further emphasizing the oppressive atmosphere.
  • 見黑龙在雲中拖尾取水 - Means 'they saw a black dragon dragging its tail to fetch water,' describing the dragon's actions.
  • 數卷后 - Means 'after several turns,' indicating the duration of the dragon's actions.
  • 洪澤湖水低三丈 - Means 'the water level of Hongze Lake dropped by three zhang,' describing the effect of the dragon's actions.
  • 人心大安 - Means 'people's hearts were greatly reassured,' indicating the relief felt by the people.
  • 龍之鱗甲金光四射 - Means 'the dragon's scales shone with golden light,' describing the dragon's appearance.
  • 惟頭身則不可見 - Means 'only the head and body were not visible,' indicating the dragon's partial visibility.
  • 此石埭縣教官沈公雨潭所目擊 - Means 'this was witnessed by Mr. Shen Yutan, the county schoolmaster of Shidai,' indicating the source of the account.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 龍 - Refers to the dragon, a significant mythical creature in Chinese culture, often associated with water and protection.
  • 高家堰 - Refers to the Gaojiayan embankment, a historical flood control structure in China.
  • 學使 - Refers to the provincial examiner, an official responsible for conducting examinations.
  • 科考 - Refers to the provincial examination, a part of the imperial examination system.
  • 淮安 - Refers to Huai'an, a city in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 河督 - Refers to the governor of the river, an official responsible for managing rivers and waterways.
  • 淮揚 - Refers to the Huaiyang region, an area in eastern China.
  • 洪澤湖 - Refers to Hongze Lake, a large lake in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 石埭縣 - Refers to Shidai County, a historical county in Anhui Province, China.
  • 教官 - Refers to the county schoolmaster, an official responsible for education in the county.

Proper Nouns

  • 乾隆二十七年 - The 27th year of the Qianlong era (1762).
  • 李公因培 - Mr. Li Yinpei.
  • 淮安 - Huai'an.
  • 高家堰 - Gaojiayan embankment.
  • 河督高公 - Governor Gao.
  • 淮揚 - Huaiyang region.
  • 洪澤湖 - Hongze Lake.
  • 石埭縣 - Shidai County.
  • 沈公雨潭 - Mr. Shen Yutan.

[826]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 雷公被污 - Colloquial expression meaning 'the thunder god was defiled' or 'the thunder god was dishonored.'
  • 淮安有雷 - Refers to a thunderstorm in Huai'an.
  • 孤贫院 - A charitable institution for the poor and orphaned.
  • 马桶 - A portable toilet, often made of wood or metal.
  • 金甲者 - Refers to a divine or supernatural being in golden armor.
  • 绕屋而下 - Means 'circled the house and descended.'
  • 雷神 - Refers to a thunder god or deity.
  • 尖嘴黑身 - Describes a creature with a pointed beak and black body.
  • 瞪目无言 - Means 'staring silently.'
  • 两翅闪闪摇动 - Describes wings that are flickering and moving.
  • 山阳县官 - Refers to the official of Shanyang County.
  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest.
  • 画符建醮 - Refers to rituals involving drawing talismans and setting up altars.
  • 清水沃其头 - Means 'poured clear water over its head.'
  • 雨 - Refers to rain.
  • 飞去 - Means 'flew away.'
  • 李文贞公梦兆 - Refers to a dream omen experienced by Li Wenzheng.
  • 祈梦于九龙滩庙 - Means 'prayed for a dream at the Nine Dragon Bay Temple.'
  • 戊戌科进士 - Refers to a scholar-official who passed the imperial examination in the year of the Horse (戊戌).
  • 宰相 - Refers to a high-ranking official or prime minister.
  • 富贵无心想 - Means 'wealth and rank are not in one's thoughts.'
  • 功名两不成 - Means 'neither merit nor reputation is achieved.'
  • 鬼求路引 - Refers to a ghost seeking a death certificate.
  • 德龄安孝廉 - Refers to a scholar-official named An Deqing.
  • 知太仓州事 - Means 'served as the magistrate of Taicang Prefecture.'
  • 幕某 - Refers to a person in the official's entourage.
  • 浙人也 - Means 'is from Zhejiang.'
  • 偶染时症 - Means 'accidentally contracted a seasonal illness.'
  • 归欤 - An interjection meaning 'return home.'
  • 无路引 - Means 'no death certificate.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 雷公 - Refers to the thunder god in Chinese mythology.
  • 孤贫院 - A charitable institution for the poor and orphaned, common in traditional Chinese society.
  • 马桶 - A portable toilet, often used in traditional settings.
  • 金甲者 - A divine or supernatural being in golden armor, often associated with deities or spirits.
  • 雷神 - A thunder god or deity, often depicted with specific physical characteristics.
  • 尖嘴黑身 - A description of a creature with a pointed beak and black body, possibly a mythological or supernatural entity.
  • 山阳县官 - Refers to the local government official in Shanyang County.
  • 道士 - A Taoist priest, often involved in religious and magical practices.
  • 画符建醮 - Rituals involving drawing talismans and setting up altars, common in Taoist and folk religious practices.
  • 戊戌科进士 - A scholar-official who passed the imperial examination in the year of the Horse (戊戌).
  • 宰相 - A high-ranking official or prime minister in the imperial government.
  • 李文贞公 - Refers to Li Wenzheng, a historical figure known for his political and literary achievements.
  • 九龙滩庙 - A temple located at Nine Dragon Bay, a site known for its spiritual significance.
  • 德龄安孝廉 - A scholar-official named An Deqing, known for his service in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 太仓州 - A prefecture in Jiangsu Province, historically significant in Chinese administration.
  • 幕某 - A person in the official's entourage, often a subordinate or assistant.

Proper Nouns

  • 雷公 - The thunder god.
  • 淮安 - Huai'an, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 孤贫院 - A charitable institution for the poor and orphaned.
  • 马桶 - A portable toilet.
  • 金甲者 - A divine or supernatural being in golden armor.
  • 雷神 - A thunder god or deity.
  • 山阳县官 - The official of Shanyang County.
  • 道士 - A Taoist priest.
  • 李文贞公 - Li Wenzheng, a historical figure.
  • 九龙滩庙 - A temple located at Nine Dragon Bay.
  • 戊戌科进士 - A scholar-official who passed the imperial examination in the year of the Horse (戊戌).
  • 宰相 - A high-ranking official or prime minister.
  • 德龄安孝廉 - An Deqing, a scholar-official.
  • 太仓州 - Taicang Prefecture, a prefecture in Jiangsu Province.
  • 幕某 - A person in the official's entourage.

[827]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 何以死于此 - Formal question meaning 'Why did you die here?' The phrase '何以' is a classical way of asking 'why' or 'for what reason.'
  • 我宁夏人，姓莫，名容非 - Formal introduction of oneself, stating 'I am from Ningxia, my surname is Mo, and my given name is Rongfei.' The structure is typical of Classical Chinese self-introductions.
  • 前太仓刺史赵酉远亲也 - Formal statement of relationship, meaning 'I am a distant relative of the former Tai仓刺史 Zhao You.' The phrase '远亲' means 'distant relative.'
  • 万里赍粮而来 - Formal statement of purpose, meaning 'I traveled thousands of miles bringing provisions.' The phrase '赍粮' means 'bringing provisions.'
  • 为投赵故 - Formal explanation of the purpose, meaning 'for the purpose of visiting Zhao.' The phrase '故' means 'for the purpose of.'
  • 赵刺史反拒不纳 - Formal statement of rejection, meaning 'the Tai仓刺史 Zhao refused to receive me.' The phrase '反拒不纳' means 'refused to receive.'
  • 且一文不赠 - Formal statement of refusal, meaning 'and did not give even a single coin.' The phrase '且' means 'and' or 'even.'
  • 故穷馁怨死于此 - Formal statement of the cause of death, meaning 'therefore, I died here of hunger and resentment.' The phrase '穷馁怨' means 'of hunger and resentment.'
  • 何以不缠赵 - Formal question meaning 'Why didn't you bother Zhao?' The phrase '缠' means 'to bother' or 'to pester.'
  • 幕友与汝宁有冤乎 - Formal question meaning 'Does the guest have a grudge against you?' The phrase '有冤' means 'has a grudge.'
  • 赵已他迁 - Formal statement of relocation, meaning 'Zhao has moved elsewhere.' The phrase '他迁' means 'moved elsewhere.'
  • 鬼无路引不能出境 - Formal statement of the supernatural, meaning 'ghosts cannot leave the realm without a travel permit.' The phrase '路引' means 'travel permit.'
  • 缠他人无益 - Formal statement of futility, meaning 'bothering others is of no use.' The phrase '无益' means 'of no use.'
  • 故来缠幕友 - Formal statement of action, meaning 'therefore, I came to bother the guest.' The phrase '故' means 'therefore.'
  • 庶几惊动主人，哀怜幕友，必与我路引 - Formal statement of hope, meaning 'hoping to arouse the master's pity and give me a travel permit.' The phrase '庶几' means 'hoping' or 'perhaps.'
  • 德公闻而许之 - Formal statement of action, meaning 'Dongong heard and agreed.' The phrase '闻而许之' means 'heard and agreed.'
  • 召吏房作文书 - Formal statement of action, meaning 'called the clerk's office to write a document.' The phrase '作文书' means 'write a document.'
  • 咨明一路河神关吏 - Formal statement of action, meaning 'consulted the river gods and customs officials along the way.' The phrase '咨明' means 'consulted' or 'informed.'
  • 放莫容非魂归故乡 - Formal statement of action, meaning 'allowed Mo Rongfei's soul to return to his hometown.' The phrase '魂归故乡' means 'soul returns to the hometown.'
  • 幕友病不医而愈 - Formal statement of recovery, meaning 'the guest's illness was cured without treatment.' The phrase '不医而愈' means 'cured without treatment.'
  • 石揆谛晖石揆、谛晖二僧 - Formal introduction of two monks, meaning 'Shikui and Dihui are two monks.' The structure is typical of Classical Chinese introductions.
  • 皆南能教也 - Formal statement of affiliation, meaning 'both are followers of the Southern School.' The phrase '南能教' refers to the Southern School of Buddhism.
  • 石揆参禅 - Formal statement of practice, meaning 'Shikui practices meditation.' The phrase '参禅' means 'practices meditation.'
  • 谛晖持戒 - Formal statement of practice, meaning 'Dihui observes precepts.' The phrase '持戒' means 'observes precepts.'
  • 两人各不相下 - Formal statement of rivalry, meaning 'the two do not yield to each other.' The phrase '不相下' means 'do not yield to each other.'
  • 谛晖住杭州灵隐寺 - Formal statement of residence, meaning 'Dihui resides at the Lingyin Temple in Hangzhou.' The phrase '住' means 'resides.'
  • 香花极盛 - Formal statement of prosperity, meaning 'the temple is very prosperous.' The phrase '香花极盛' means 'very prosperous.'
  • 石揆谋夺之 - Formal statement of intent, meaning 'Shikui planned to take it over.' The phrase '谋夺之' means 'planned to take it over.'
  • 会天竺祈雨 - Formal statement of coincidence, meaning 'just as the Tianzhu Temple was praying for rain.' The phrase '会' means 'just as' or 'coincidentally.'
  • 石揆持咒召黑龙行雨 - Formal statement of action, meaning 'Shikui chanted a spell to summon the black dragon to bring rain.' The phrase '持咒召' means 'chanted a spell to summon.'
  • 人共见之 - Formal statement of observation, meaning 'people all saw it.' The phrase '共见之' means 'all saw it.'
  • 以为神 - Formal statement of perception, meaning 'they thought it was a miracle.' The phrase '以为神' means 'thought it was a miracle.'
  • 谛晖闻知 - Formal statement of knowledge, meaning 'Dihui heard about it.' The phrase '闻知' means 'heard about.'
  • 即避去 - Formal statement of action, meaning 'immediately left.' The phrase '即避去' means 'immediately left.'
  • 隐云栖最僻处 - Formal statement of location, meaning 'hid in the most secluded part of Yunqi.' The phrase '隐' means 'hid.'
  • 石揆为灵隐长老 - Formal statement of position, meaning 'Shikui became the abbot of Lingyin Temple.' The phrase '为长老' means 'became the abbot.'
  • 垂三十年 - Formal statement of duration, meaning 'for nearly thirty years.' The phrase '垂' means 'nearly' or 'approaching.'
  • 身本万历孝廉 - Formal statement of background, meaning 'originally a scholar-official of the Wanli era.' The phrase '身本' means 'originally.'
  • 口若悬河 - Formal statement of eloquence, meaning 'speaks eloquently.' The phrase '口若悬河' means 'speaks eloquently.'
  • 灵隐兰若之会 - Formal statement of event, meaning 'the gathering at Lingyin Temple.' The phrase '兰若之会' means 'gathering at the temple.'
  • 震动一时 - Formal statement of impact, meaning 'caused a sensation at the time.' The phrase '震动一时' means 'caused a sensation at the time.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宁夏 - A province in northwestern China, historically significant for its strategic location and cultural heritage.
  • 太仓刺史 - A high-ranking official position in ancient China, responsible for administrative and judicial duties.
  • 赵酉 - A historical figure, likely a notable official or scholar from the Wanli era.
  • 天竺祈雨 - Refers to the practice of praying for rain at the Tianzhu Temple, a significant Buddhist temple in Hangzhou.
  • 灵隐寺 - A famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 云栖 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in Hangzhou.
  • 万历孝廉 - Refers to a scholar-official from the Wanli era, a period of the Ming Dynasty.
  • 南能教 - Refers to the Southern School of Buddhism, a major school of Chan Buddhism.
  • 参禅 - A practice of meditation and contemplation in Buddhism.
  • 持戒 - Observing precepts, a fundamental practice in Buddhism.
  • 兰若 - A term for a Buddhist temple or hermitage.
  • 震动一时 - A phrase indicating that something caused a significant impact or sensation at the time.

[828]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有沈氏儿丧父母 - Narrative about a young boy from the Shen family who lost his parents.
  • 为人佣工 - Worked as a laborer for others, indicating his early life circumstances.
  • 随施主入寺 - Followed a benefactor into the temple, suggesting a connection to a religious setting.
  • 石揆见之大惊 - Shi Kui saw him and was greatly surprised, indicating a strong reaction.
  • 愿乞此儿为弟子 - Wished to adopt this child as a disciple, showing Shi Kui's desire to take the boy under his wing.
  • 施主许之 - The benefactor agreed, indicating consent.
  • 儿方七岁 - The child was just seven years old, specifying his age.
  • 即为延师教读 - Immediately hired a teacher to educate him, showing Shi Kui's commitment to the boy's education.
  • 儿欲肉食，即与之肉 - If the child wanted meat, it was given to him, indicating indulgence.
  • 儿欲衣绣，即衣之绣 - If the child wanted embroidered clothes, he was given them, further showing indulgence.
  • 不削发也 - He did not shave his head, indicating that the boy was not fully initiated into monastic life.
  • 儿亦聪颖，通举子业 - The child was also intelligent and proficient in the studies required for the imperial examinations, highlighting his academic abilities.
  • 年将冠矣 - He was about to reach adulthood, indicating his age.
  • 督学某考杭州 - The supervisor of education in Hangzhou, indicating a specific official.
  • 令儿应考，取名近思 - Ordered the child to take the exam and named him Jinsi, showing the official's involvement.
  • 遂取中府学第三名 - He was ranked third in the prefectural school, indicating his success.
  • 命跪佛前剃其发 - Ordered him to kneel before the Buddha and shave his head, showing a change in status.
  • 披以袈裟 - Dressed him in a monk's robe, further indicating his new status.
  • 同学诸生闻之大怒 - The fellow students heard of it and were greatly angry, indicating a strong reaction.
  • 连名数百人上控巡抚学院 - Joined names and petitioned the governor and the school superintendent, showing a collective action.
  • 有项霜泉者，仁和学霜也 - There was Xiang Shuangquan, the head of the Renshou school, introducing a key figure.
  • 率家僮数十篡取近思 - Led dozens of servants to forcibly take Jinsi, showing a dramatic action.
  • 为假辫以饰之 - Made a fake queue to cover his head, indicating an attempt to disguise him.
  • 即以己妹配之 - Immediately married his own sister to him, showing a personal connection.
  • 置酒作乐，聚三学弟子员赋《催妆诗》作贺 - Held a banquet and made music, gathering students from three schools to compose a *Cui Zhuang* poem to congratulate, indicating a celebratory event.
  • 诸大府虽与石揆交，而众怒难犯 - The officials, though they were friends with Shi Kui, found it difficult to face the public anger, showing a conflict between personal relationships and public pressure.
  • 不得已，准诸生所控 - Reluctantly, they allowed the students' petition, indicating a compromise.
  • 许近思蓄发为儒 - Allowed Jinsi to keep his hair and be a Confucian scholar, showing the resolution.
  • 诸生犹不服，各汹汹然 - The students were still不服, each looking very angry, indicating ongoing dissatisfaction.
  • 欲焚灵隐寺殴石揆 - Wanted to burn Lingyin Temple and beat Shi Kui, showing the intensity of their anger.
  • 大府不得已，取石揆两侍者，各笞十五 - The officials, unable to do otherwise, took two of Shi Kui's attendants and each beat them fifteen times, showing a punitive action.
  • 群忿始息 - The public anger finally subsided, indicating the end of the conflict.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 沈氏 - Refers to a family name, indicating the boy's background.
  • 石揆 - Refers to a monk or religious figure, indicating a key character in the story.
  • 施主 - Refers to a benefactor or patron, indicating a person who supports the temple or religious activities.
  • 杭州 - Refers to Hangzhou, a major city in Zhejiang Province, indicating a specific location.
  • 府学 - Refers to a prefectural school, indicating an educational institution.
  • 举子业 - Refers to the studies required for the imperial examinations, indicating the academic focus.
  • 灵隐寺 - Refers to Lingyin Temple, a famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou, indicating a significant religious site.
  • 催妆诗 - Refers to a type of poem traditionally composed to congratulate a bridegroom, indicating a cultural practice.

Proper Nouns

  • 沈氏 - The family name of the boy.
  • 石揆 - The name of the monk or religious figure.
  • 施主 - The benefactor or patron.
  • 杭州 - The city of Hangzhou.
  • 府学 - The prefectural school.
  • 灵隐寺 - Lingyin Temple.
  • 催妆诗 - The type of poem traditionally composed to congratulate a bridegroom.

[829]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后一月 - Time expression meaning 'one month later'.
  • 石揆命侍者撞钟鼓召集合寺僧 - Formal command structure where Shi Kui orders his attendants to strike the bells and drums to assemble all the monks of the temple.
  • 各持香一炷礼佛毕 - Ritual action where each monk holds a stick of incense and finishes worshipping Buddha.
  • 泣曰 - Phrase indicating that someone is crying and speaking.
  • 此予负谛晖之报也 - Self-reflection statement meaning 'This is my retribution for wronging Di Hui'.
  • 灵隐本谛晖所住地 - Statement about the original residence of Di Hui at Lingyin Temple.
  • 予以一念争胜之心夺之 - Reflection on the speaker's competitive mindset that led to taking over Di Hui's place.
  • 念己身灭度后 - Phrase about the speaker's thoughts after death.
  • 非有大福分人，不能撑持此地 - Statement that only someone with great fortune can maintain the place.
  • 沈氏儿风骨严整 - Description of Shen's son having a dignified and upright character.
  • 在人间为一品官，在佛家为罗汉身 - Comparison of Shen's son's status in secular and religious contexts.
  • 故余见而倾心 - Statement about the speaker being deeply impressed by Shen's son.
  • 欲以此坐与之 - Desire to give this position to Shen's son.
  • 又一念争胜 - Another competitive thought.
  • 欲使佛法胜于孔子 - Desire to make Buddhism superior to Confucianism.
  • 故先使入学 - Decision to first send Shen's son to school.
  • 以继我孝廉出身之衣钵 - Continuation of the speaker's scholarly lineage.
  • 贪嗔未灭之客气也 - Recognition of unextinguished greed and anger.
  • 今侍儿受杖 - Statement about the servant being punished.
  • 为辱已甚 - Feeling of great shame.
  • 尚何面目坐方丈乎 - Question about the speaker's face to sit in the abbot's quarters.
  • 夫儒家之改过，即佛家之忏悔也 - Statement equating Confucian repentance with Buddhist confession.
  • 自今以往，吾将赴释梵天王处忏悔百年 - Vow to confess for a hundred years in front of the Buddha and the celestial kings.
  • 才能得道 - Condition for achieving enlightenment.
  • 诸弟子速持我禅杖一枝，白玉钵盂一个、紫衣袈裟一袭往迎谛晖 - Instruction to the disciples to bring specific items to welcome Di Hui.
  • 为我补过 - Request to make up for the speaker's mistakes.
  • 群僧合掌跪泣曰 - Scene where the monks join hands and cry.
  • 谛晖逃出已三十年 - Statement that Di Hui has been missing for thirty years.
  • 音耗寂然 - Description of no news or communication.
  • 从何地迎接 - Question about where to find Di Hui.
  • 现在云栖第几山第几寺 - Specific location mentioned.
  • 户外有松一株、井一口 - Descriptions of a pine tree and a well outside the temple.
  • 汝第记此去访可也 - Instruction to remember these details and visit.
  • 言毕，趺坐而逝 - Description of the speaker sitting in lotus position and passing away.
  • 鼻垂玉柱二尺许 - Physical detail about the speaker's nose.
  • 群僧如其言，果得谛晖 - Statement that the monks followed the instructions and found Di Hui.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 石揆 - Shi Kui, a historical figure, likely a high-ranking monk or abbot.
  • 谛晖 - Di Hui, another historical figure, likely a prominent monk.
  • 灵隐 - Lingyin Temple, a famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou, China.
  • 方丈 - The abbot's quarters in a Buddhist temple.
  • 释梵天王 - The Buddha and the celestial kings, important figures in Buddhism.
  • 云栖 - Yunxi, a place name, possibly a mountain or temple.
  • 禅杖 - A walking stick used by Buddhist monks.
  • 白玉钵盂 - A white jade bowl, a sacred object in Buddhism.
  • 紫衣袈裟 - A purple robe and cassock, traditional Buddhist attire.
  • 孝廉 - A Confucian scholar-official, a term from the imperial examination system.
  • 贪嗔 - Greed and anger, two of the six poisons in Buddhism.
  • 改过 - Repentance, a concept in Confucian ethics.
  • 忏悔 - Confession, a practice in Buddhism.
  • 罗汉 - Arhat, a stage of enlightenment in Buddhism.
